purpose: Students identify what they want to remember from M10. Planning-focused reflection appropriate for this age (8-9 year olds stronger at "what did I learn?" than "how is my understanding changing?").
visual: Clean display. No equations visible (reflective space).
guide: "If you had to remember one thing from today, what would it be?"
options:
A: "Equals means same value as"
B: "The unknown can go anywhere"
C: "Different pictures can have the same equation"
responses:
**A:** "Same value as — that's the equals sign's job. You'll use that in every equation from now on."
**B:** "Unknowns move, but the relationship stays. You can figure out any missing piece."
**C:** "Rows, stacks, groups — different pictures, same math underneath."
design_note: Each option maps to one M10 demand. No wrong answers — all are valid takeaways. Option A (= sign) is the most foundational; Option B (unknowns) is the most novel; Option C (variety) is the most transferable.  Guide response is ONE sentence per option — brief validation without over-explaining. Data from student selections could inform which M10 concept resonated most (or least), useful for Practice calibration in future iterations.
